title: Use data in column for link
excerpt:
Hi, I have an ID in a column I’d like to use for another column (Link column). So e.g. the first column says 12345 and then the link would use this to have this url: https://mysite.com/12345. 
Is that doable?

posts.author: TEAMVAERK (Morten)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, I have an ID in a column I’d like to use for another column (Link column). So e.g. the first column says 12345 and then the link would use this to have this url: https://mysite.com/12345.

Is that doable?

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @TEAMVAERK

When you enter a https:// address in a normal text column it will show up as a link. Not as nice as the link column itself (you can’t give it a name) but the advantage is that you can use the AutoID Column app from the marketplace and generate an id that looks like:
https://excellent-team.nl/{id}

I don’t know if your ID’s on your site are in a range that every id is +1, but you can certainly use the AutoID app to give you a solution. You can also use query vars, like:

https://excellent-team.nl/?myvar={id}

posts.author: TEAMVAERK (Morten)
posts.cookedHtml:
basdebruin:

AutoID Column

Thanks basdebruin! Its a 37$ app, so might be better to just use a different connection we already have, if some external is needed. But its a nice to know!
